etymology.description: 艹 ('plant') + 盧 ('phonetic') -> plant with hollow stems ('reed; rush')
etymology.interpretation: The character 蘆 combines the semantic 艹 (艹, 'plant') with the phonetic component 盧 (盧, 'phonetic') to depict a plant, specifically reeds or rushes, known for their hollow stems.
etymology.simplification: 蘆(蘆) was simplified to 芦(蘆) by retaining 艹 (艹, 'plant') and replacing 盧 (盧) with simpler components representing sound and meaning.

examples.chinese: 湖边长满了芦苇(湖邊長滿了蘆葦)。
examples.pinyin: Hú biān zhǎng mǎn le lúwěi.
examples.english: The lakeside is full of reeds.

examples.chinese: 他用芦杆(他用蘆杆)做了一个风筝(做了一個風箏)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng lǔgǎn zuò le yī gè fēngzhēng.
examples.english: He made a kite using reed stalks.

examples.chinese: 芦笛的声音(蘆笛的聲音)在夜晚很悦耳(在夜晚很悅耳)。
examples.pinyin: Lúdí de shēngyīn zài yèwǎn hěn yuè'ěr.
examples.english: The sound of the reed flute is very pleasant at night.
